description.value:
Used as a replacement for skills rolled with base COOL, EMP, or WILL

Used for rolling in checks which involve force of will, charisma, general intelligence, or intution. Note that if the check is specialized and the mook is not, the DV should be raised 1-3 tiers.

Used as a replacement for fighting or ranged weapon skills, excluding evasion. Note that this uses REF as a base for reasons involving initiative - set REF of the mook first, then consider that number when considering the Martial skill.
Used as a replacement for skills which rely on base Body or Dex, which are not covered by the Martial skill, and for Evasion.
Used for skills which rely on base INT or TECH.

A basic lob or pitch of an object. Use an Action to make a Ranged Attack at up to 25m/yds. Targets can choose to Dodge your throw if they can Dodge bullets. Melee weapons deal their base damage, but don't penetrate half SP.
Deals no damage. If hit roll DV15 Resist Torture/Drugs. On a fail take the Damaged Eye and Damaged Ear Critical injuries (see pg. 188) for 1 minute. No Crit damage dealt.
A heavy SMG. Due to its size it can't be concealed.
Light version of an Armorjack. No armor penalty.
Light version of an Armorjack for your head. No armor penalty.

Common ammunition often used as a cheap substitute for armor-piercing rounds. Each round contains a soft, reactive core designed to splatter on a target rather than penetrate it. Deals 1d6 damage less than basic ammunition of the same type, but always ablates the target's armor by the listed amount.

information.notes:
Mook Tier - Tier 3/Rank-And-File - Low-level but accredited members of NC's various groups, with enough authority to wear the org's colors but not enough to pose a real threat on their own.

Gang-Wide Ability - Guerilla Tactics: 6th Street gang members can enter and leave partial cover without a penalty, and do not suffer penalties while firing out of it.

Ability - Pin Them Down: When an enemy moves towards a corporal that is currently in half cover, the corporal can immediately attempt the suppressive fire action.

For every 3 6th Streeters in a scene, 1 Corporal is equipped with a flashbang and AP grenade.

Privates are the first on the scene, but corporals pin down the targets with suppressive fire, and soften them up with grenades.
